messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agent Host: Agent can't see images returned by Integrated Browser screenshots tool ISSUE BODY: **To repro** - Enable these settings: ``` "workbench.browser.agentHostChatToolsEnabled": true, "workbench.browser.enableChatTools": true, "chat.agentHost.enabled": true, ``` - Start an Agent Host local session. (for verifier: if you don't know how to do that, follow [this TPI](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/313802)) - Type the prompt: ``` Open apple.com, take a screenshot, and tell me the output of the screenshot tool ``` > [!IMPORTANT] > **NOTE added on May 14:** Please make sure to use this exact same prompt, since if you don't ask explicitly about the output of the screenshot tool, it will hallucinate what it thinks is in the output based on the text-based ARIA snapshot provided by other browser tools. - 🐛 It says that the tool returned no output, despite the screenshot appearing in the chat: --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Manual fallback If the launch skill cannot be used, the same scenario is achievable by hand: open Agents window, send the same prompts, inspect the chat UI, then `tail -f` the agentHost log under `~/.config/Code - Insiders/logs/.../agentHost.log` (path varies by OS; `code-oss-logs` resolves it). --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Quick Start with Aspire Dashboard To collect agent host traces with the [Aspire Dashboard](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire/fundamentals/dashboard/standalone) (or any OTLP-compatible collector): ```json { "chat.agentHost.enabled": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.enabled": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.captureContent": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.dbSpanExporter.enabled": true, "chat.agentHost.otel.otlpEndpoint": "http://localhost:4318" } ``` This combination persists every span to SQLite for offline inspection **and** forwards them live to Aspire — useful when you want to spot-check a session in the dashboard and still be able to query the raw data later. --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### 4. View Traces Open http://localhost:18888 → **Traces**. You'll see `invoke_agent` spans with nested `chat` and `execute_tool` children. ![Screenshot showing agent interaction traces in the Aspire Dashboard with spans for invoke_agent, chat, and execute_tool.](../media/trace-aspire-dashboard.png) --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host system-prompt customization This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host** (ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's `extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agent Host: Fix image output visibility from Integrated Browser screenshots tool",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "browser-tools", "image-output", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As an Agent Host user, I want the screenshot tool to properly display and report image output in chat responses so that users can see both the visual screenshot AND receive confirmation that the tool successfully returned image data.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a prompt asking for a screenshot AND its output (e.g., 'Open apple.com, take a screenshot, and tell me the output of the screenshot tool'), When the Agent Host executes the browser screenshot tool, Then the response should include both: 1) The actual screenshot image displayed in chat, 2) A text confirmation stating that the screenshot tool returned an image",
    "Given the screenshot tool returns base64 or binary image data, When this data is passed through the Agent Host Protocol, Then the agent host correctly identifies and forwards this as valid tool output (not 'no output')",
    "Given traces are enabled with OTLP export, When inspecting invoke_agent spans, Then nested execute_tool children should show successful screenshot execution with image payload present",
    "Given the chat UI displays responses, When a screenshot tool is used, Then the response message must explicitly indicate that an image was returned by the tool"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate Agent Host Protocol to understand how binary/image payloads are serialized and transmitted",
      "Add logging in screenshot tool execution path to capture full response including image data",
      "Modify agent host response builder to properly include image output from tools when requested",
      "Update system prompt handling to recognize and forward non-text tool outputs correctly"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Verify chat UI correctly renders both image content AND text confirmation of tool output",
      "Add visual indicator in chat responses showing 'Screenshot returned' status message"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Reproduce issue with exact prompt: 'Open apple.com, take a screenshot, and tell me the output of the screenshot tool'",
      "Verify screenshot image appears in chat response",
      "Verify text confirmation stating screenshot was returned is present",
      "Check Agent Host logs for any errors during screenshot execution",
      "Validate traces show execute_tool span with image payload"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Bug fixed - screenshots from browser tools are properly reported as tool output in agent host responses",
    "Test cases added and passing",
    "Code reviewed by backend and frontend team members",
    "Traces verified showing correct image payload handling"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the exact format of the screenshot tool's return value (base64 string, binary blob, URL)?",
    "Is this a regression or new issue? Was it working previously?",
    "Are there any other browser tools that return non-text outputs (PDFs, tables as images) that might be affected?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The screenshot tool returns image data in a standard format supported by the Agent Host Protocol",
    "The issue is specifically about reporting/acknowledging the output rather than displaying the image itself",
    "Agent Host logs are accessible at ~/.config/Code - Insiders/logs/agentHost.log"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host system-prompt customization section",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Manual fallback and trace viewing instructions",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Aspire Dashboard tracing configuration"
  ]
}
```
